entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: the word sits buddhistha, in the intellect, complete and unsounded

entities.name: buddhi
entities.cite: the word residing in the intellect is, separately, the cause of audible sounds

entities.name: śruti
entities.cite: articulation churns it into audible sruti, and the sounds then do the manifesting

entities.name: agni
entities.cite: Fire is latent in the arani, the fire-stick, before any kindling

entities.name: araṇi
entities.cite: fire that resides in the kindling-wood

entities.name: jvala
entities.cite: churned, it becomes flame, which then illuminates other things

entities.name: prakīrya
entities.cite: articulation churns it into audible sruti

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: the word sits buddhistha, in the intellect, complete and unsounded

axioms.name: karana
axioms.cite: the inner word is a cause of the sounds without being identical to them

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: Fire is latent in the arani, the fire-stick, before any kindling

axioms.name: karana
axioms.cite: churned, it becomes flame, which then illuminates other things

axioms.name: parinamate_deny
axioms.cite: The flame is not created by the churning ex nihilo — it was in the wood in potentia

axioms.name: asananta
axioms.cite: the sounds do not create the word; they release and transmit what was already whole within the speaker

denials.cite: the inner word is a cause of the sounds without being identical to them, as fire-in-wood is not the visible blaze
fire-in-wood is not the visible blaze

denials.name: parinamate
denials.cite: it was in the wood in potentia

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: the word residing in the intellect

claims.name: karana
claims.cite: is, separately, the cause of audible sounds

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: As the fire that resides in the kindling-wood

claims.cite: is the cause of further illumination

rejected_readings.label: parinama_transformation
rejected_readings.rendering: The word transforms into sounds through articulation.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts parinama (real transformation of substance) rather than vivarta (appearance without loss of unity). The commentary explicitly denies this: 'The flame is not created by the churning ex nihilo — it was in the wood in potentia.' The word remains whole in the speaker; articulation releases it without constituting transformation.

rejected_readings.label: identity_sabda_sruti
rejected_readings.rendering: The word just is the audible sounds, manifested through articulation.
rejected_readings.why: Collapses the distinction the verse insists on: 'separately' (prthak) guards that inner word and sounds are not identical, 'as fire-in-wood is not the visible blaze.' The word has potentiality without identity to its manifestations.

rejected_readings.label: sounds_produce_word
rejected_readings.rendering: The audible sounds produce or generate the word from scratch.
rejected_readings.why: Inverts causation backward. The commentary makes clear: 'the sounds do not create the word; they release and transmit what was already whole within the speaker.' The word is latent in the speaker's intellect before uttering; sounds manifest it without constituting it.

rejected_readings.label: sabda_as_property
rejected_readings.rendering: The word-property manifests as audible sounds.
rejected_readings.why: Sort error: demotes sabda from absolute (unchanging principle) to property. The entire simile structure depends on sabda being a constant, unmodified principle latent in the speaker, like fire latent in the wood — not a variable property.

notes: The verse deploys fire-in-wood as simile to model word-in-intellect: both latent unmanifested principles whose manifestations (flame, sounds) do not transform the principles themselves and remain distinct from them. The 'separately' (prthak) of the verse is key: sabda causes sruti without identity. The deny of parinama and backward causation guards against two typical confusions: thinking manifestation is transformation, and thinking sounds generate rather than express the word.
